calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions To Trustee To Determine Validity Of The Harrison Parker Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Maureen McFadden for and with Timothy Breen (Guadrian Ad Litem).
James O'Brien for Petitioner.

The Court raised the issue of conflict of interest. Mr. O'Brien stated that he believes a Guardian Ad Litem has been appointed and addresses the issue of conflict of interest. Mr. Breen stated that he has no objection to the petition, that he has spoken with the Minors, that they have a complete understanding and are not objecting, and that they agree to move forward. The Court granted the Petition for Instructions to Determine Validity of the Revocable Trust (subject to review of the order). Mr O'Brien will provide a form of order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions To Trustee To Determine Validity Of The Harrison Parker Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Jun-10-2019 Continued To Jul-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions To Trustee To Determine Validity Of The Harrison Parker Revocable Trust
Off Calendar May-13-2019 Continued To Jun-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Continued To May-13-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions To Trustee To Determine Validity Of The Harrison Parker Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Mar-20-2019 Continued To Apr-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions To Trustee To Determine Validity Of The Harrison Parker Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Continued To Mar-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James O'Brien for Harrison Parker, Jr. (Petitioner/Trustee).

Mr. O'Brien addressed the issue of the Trustee's conflict of interest stating that when the Trustee is presented with information it is his duty to carry out the wishes of the Settlors, that he brought that information to the Court and that it is the best the Trustee can do. Mr. O'Brien also stated that there is no conflict of interest with regard to the Guardians, that there were two bank accounts, that the first one was a joint savings account at Bank of America with a negative balance of $4.95 at time of death so the account lapsed, that the Great Western account was also a joint account and was no longer at that financial institution at time of death, and that neither account had assets and it could have been withdrawn at any time. The Court stated that a Guardian Ad Litem needs to be appointed for the Grandchildren to see if they agree with this amendment. The Petition for Instructions to Trustee to Determine Validity of The Harrison Parker Revocable Trust is continued to March 20, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported..
